axes.definition: The human actor or bounded operator mode whose job to be done drives the surface.
axes.why_it_matters: Prevents patient, clinician, support, hub, pharmacy, operations, governance, and assistive users from collapsing into one generic actor.

axes.definition: The published visibility and mutation ceiling resolved through AudienceVisibilityCoverage and MinimumNecessaryContract.
axes.why_it_matters: Separates who the actor is from what data and actions the current surface is lawfully allowed to expose.

axes.definition: The owning persistent shell family that preserves continuity for the active entity and route family.
axes.why_it_matters: Stops route prefixes, local layouts, or feature labels from impersonating a different shell family.

axes.definition: The posture adaptation of the owning shell: browser, embedded, or constrained_browser.
axes.why_it_matters: Lets embedded or constrained delivery narrow chrome and capability without redefining shell ownership.

axes.definition: The channel by which the actor enters or resumes the lineage: web, embedded, telephony, secure-link, or support-assisted capture.
axes.why_it_matters: Preserves telephony parity and same-lineage continuation without creating a second back-office workflow.

axes.definition: The routed surface family that claims shell residency and owns the dominant action and continuity rules.
axes.why_it_matters: Makes route ownership explicit so /workspace, /ops, /hub, and patient portal sections cannot belong to two shells at once.

base_audience_tiers.definition: Public-safe patient status, secure-link entry before proof, and governed recovery only.
Signed-in patient routes with richer request, booking, message, record, pharmacy, and document visibility after live checks pass.
Practice-owned operational and clinically necessary detail for the current organisation only.
Coordination-safe cross-site visibility for ranked offers, travel constraints, and practice-ack debt, without full clinical narrative.
Service-delivery detail required to fulfil the booked or referred service for the site in scope.
Masked summary, chronology, and consequence-preview detail, with stronger gates for identity or access-affecting work.

derived_audience_tiers.reason: The corpus distinguishes grant-scoped recovery posture without minting a separate Phase 0 base tier.
Embedded patient use is a channel-constrained authenticated posture, not a second shell family.
Separates clinician/designated reviewer work from generic practice ops while preserving the same base visibility tier.
Keeps practice-operational actors distinct from clinical reviewers inside the shared workspace shell.

derived_audience_tiers.coverage_basis: purpose-of-use-specific control-plane rows for operations surfaces
derived_audience_tiers.reason: Operations is a control-room specialization that must not borrow ordinary practice, hub, or support payloads.
Governance/admin/config/comms/access work is a distinct control surface, not an operations subpanel.

derived_audience_tiers.coverage_basis: AssistiveSurfaceBinding bound to the owning shell and current audience coverage
derived_audience_tiers.reason: Assistive use is an adjunct posture inside the owning task unless the corpus explicitly calls for standalone evaluation or replay tooling.

assumptions.statement: Grant-scoped patient recovery is modeled as a derived audience tier over Phase 0 patient_public coverage because the corpus distinguishes secure-link recovery by purpose of use rather than by adding a third mandatory patient base tier.

Telephony/IVR and secure-link continuation are mapped to constrained_browser shell posture where a current shell is preserved, because Phase 0 canonicalizes browser, embedded, and constrained_browser as the only shell channel profiles.
Pre-submit intake and standalone assistive-control route families are inventory labels derived from the corpus until later tasks publish concrete URL contracts.

risks.statement: Seq_005 must harden explicit intake and secure-link route contracts so later API and projection work does not drift between derived labels and implementation endpoints.
Operations, governance, assurance, and break-glass audience rows are clearly required by Phase 0 but are not yet centralized in one dedicated machine-readable coverage registry.

Standalone assistive evaluation, replay, monitoring, and release-control tooling is named by function in the corpus, so any later concrete routes must preserve the bounded-secondary rule encoded here.

personas.identity_posture: Anonymous or partially identified patient before claim or sign-in uplift.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Public-safe summary, placeholder, and governed recovery only until richer proof exists.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Describe the issue and submit it into the governed intake pipeline.
Upload optional evidence and contact preferences without leaving the intake lineage.
allowed_mutations: Submit the intake for governed promotion.
continuity_expectations: Stay on one intake lineage even if the channel later changes to secure link, telephony, or support-assisted capture.
degraded_recovery_states: Fallback review, artifact quarantine, safe receipt, or later claim recovery inside the same lineage.
personas.notes: This persona covers public self-service form entry, not post-submit patient portal actions.

personas.identity_posture: Authenticated self-service session with live binding, release, and visibility checks.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Signed-in patient detail, threads, records, documents, and manage actions under VisibilityProjectionPolicy.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Track requests, appointments, communications, and records inside one signed-in shell.
Perform route-bound post-submit actions only when current capability and settlement truth allows it.
allowed_mutations: Reply to messages or more-info prompts.
Manage appointments and route-bound follow-up actions.
continuity_expectations: Keep the same patient shell across Home, Requests, Appointments, Health record, and Messages.
degraded_recovery_states: Read-only, placeholder, bounded recovery, or safe-browser handoff without losing the current shell anchor.

personas.sub_persona: Grant-scoped patient resuming a specific lineage
personas.identity_posture: Minimal access grant or verified continuation before full authenticated uplift.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Summary-only or suppressed-recovery-only visibility until grant redemption and route checks pass.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Resume one specific request, message, callback, or booking/manage path without broadening the shell to a full authenticated portal.
Step up into richer self-service only when current grant, route, and publication truth still match.
allowed_mutations: Trigger claim, re-auth, contact repair, or bounded route resumption.
continuity_expectations: Preserve the same request or child-route anchor across secure-link redemption, step-up, and recovery.
degraded_recovery_states: Expired link recovery, placeholder-only posture, safe-browser handoff, or identity hold.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm
personas.notes: This is deliberately separated from the authenticated portal persona because the corpus gives secure-link recovery its own purpose-of-use law.

personas.identity_posture: Phone-origin demand with governed patient identification or partial identity capture.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: The same public-safe intake and downstream status model as digital entry, without a parallel back-office workflow.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Start or continue intake through telephony/IVR with parity to digital intake.
Receive optional SMS continuation without changing the underlying request lineage.
allowed_mutations: Append SubmissionIngressRecord rows through telephony capture.
continuity_expectations: Preserve one SubmissionEnvelope and later same-shell continuity across phone, SMS continuation, and digital recovery.
degraded_recovery_states: Telephony outage, delivery dispute, or governed fallback review without spawning a separate support-only workflow.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0.2 Continuity key and shell law

personas.identity_posture: Authenticated patient session running under embedded manifest, release, and bridge-capability checks.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: The same patient shell under tighter bridge and publication controls, not a second app.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Use the patient portal through a trusted embedded channel without losing same-shell continuity.
Perform embedded-safe versions of ordinary patient actions when release and bridge posture permit.
allowed_mutations: The same route-bound patient mutations as the browser portal, but only after embedded validation passes.
continuity_expectations: Preserve the same patient shell while channel profile, chrome, and delivery options narrow.
degraded_recovery_states: Read-only, placeholder, safe-browser handoff, or channel-freeze recovery without shell replacement.
personas.notes: Deferred baseline expansion only; this inventory tracks it without reclassifying it as current scope.

personas.sub_persona: Clinician or designated reviewer in Clinical Workspace
personas.identity_posture: Authenticated staff actor operating with current acting scope, review lease, and queue/task continuity.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Practice-owned clinical detail and decision context only for the current organisation and scope.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Scan the queue, open the right task, and reach a safe decision quickly.
Use bounded assistive and evidence surfaces without losing task continuity.
allowed_mutations: Request more information, commit decisions, or route downstream work under lease and settlement control.
continuity_expectations: Same request lineage, same shell; queue switches and child review states preserve the active task anchor.
degraded_recovery_states: Observe-only, reassigned, stale-recoverable, or recovery-required posture that preserves the last safe task summary.
external_dependency_touchpoints: NHS login downstream only through patient-facing child work

personas.identity_posture: Authenticated staff actor with practice-scoped operational visibility and bounded mutation authority.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Practice-owned operational detail without hub, support, or cross-organisation payload broadening after assembly.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Work queue items, admin resolution, and operational follow-up in the same workspace shell.
Escalate, hand off, or resume tasks without leaving the queue/task continuity frame.
allowed_mutations: Claim tasks, send more-info, complete admin-resolution work, and move items to downstream flows when allowed.
continuity_expectations: Keep queue filters, selected anchors, and last safe posture while switching between operational tasks.
degraded_recovery_states: Observe-only, blocked, or recovery-required task posture when scope, trust, or publication drifts.

personas.identity_posture: Cross-organisation hub desk actor operating under acting context, ownership lease, and coordination policy evaluation.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Coordination-safe summary, ranked offers, practice-ack debt, and callback or return posture without full narrative by default.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Coordinate cross-site booking or callback fallback from one hub shell.
Keep origin-practice continuity visible while alternatives, callback, or return-to-practice work progresses.
allowed_mutations: Claim hub cases, refresh candidates, offer alternatives, commit native booking, or return to practice.
continuity_expectations: Preserve the selected case anchor and same hub shell across queue, case detail, alternatives, exceptions, and audit child work.
degraded_recovery_states: Confirmation-pending, practice-ack debt, callback-transfer pending, stale-owner recovery, or identity-repair hold without detaching from the active case.

personas.identity_posture: Servicing-site actor working a pharmacy case inside the dedicated pharmacy mission frame.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Only the detail needed to deliver, validate, hand off, or assure the booked or referred service for that pharmacy case.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Validate, fulfil, hand off, and assure Pharmacy First work without falling back to generic workspace layout.
Keep settlement and continuity truth attached to the same pharmacy case shell.
allowed_mutations: Validate, inventory-check, resolve, hand off, or assurance-review within the active pharmacy case.
continuity_expectations: Deep links, refresh, browser history, and reconcile recovery reopen the current pharmacy shell and selected anchor.
degraded_recovery_states: Read-only or recovery posture when runtime binding, settlement chain, or continuity evidence drifts.

personas.identity_posture: Masked-support actor with explicit disclosure ceilings, action leases, and replay or observe gates.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Masked chronology and consequence preview by default, with stronger gates for identity correction, secure-link reissue, and replay.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Operate a ticket-centric repair workspace across resend, restore, identity, reachability, and handoff work.
Assist capture into the same request lineage without turning support into a parallel workflow system.
allowed_mutations: Controlled resend, link reissue, attachment recovery, identity correction, and support-assisted capture under lease.
continuity_expectations: Stay on one support shell while moving between ticket, conversation, history, knowledge, action, observe, and replay child routes.
degraded_recovery_states: Read-only fallback, replay restore, pending-external hold, or stale reacquire while preserving the current ticket anchor and mask scope.

personas.identity_posture: Control-room actor working inside a board-scoped shell with selection leases, delta gates, and intervention fences.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Cross-surface, PHI-adjacent operational visibility bound to purpose-of-use-specific coverage rows, not ordinary staff payloads.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Detect bottlenecks and decide where bounded intervention has the highest safe leverage.
Drill into anomalies without losing board state, selection, or return context.
allowed_mutations: Issue operations interventions, freeze or compare board state, and hand off to governance when required.
continuity_expectations: Reuse the same operations shell while filters, selected anomaly, horizon, and drill path stay within the board continuity frame.
degraded_recovery_states: Read-only diagnostic, partial restore, stale selection freeze, or governance handoff posture without discarding the last stable board.

personas.sub_persona: Governance, admin, config, comms, or access lead
personas.identity_posture: Governance actor operating with scope token, baseline snapshot, freeze disposition, and approval package continuity.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Purpose-of-use-specific governance visibility that must not be reconstructed from ordinary ops or staff shells.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Review, approve, promote, rollback, configure, and audit governance work inside a dedicated shell.
Pivot to read-only evidence without losing the current governed object, draft, or watch context.
allowed_mutations: Approve, promote, rollback, manage access, update config, govern communications, and review records holds within the current review package.
continuity_expectations: Keep one governance continuity frame across diff, impact, simulation, approval, promotion watch, and evidence pivots.
degraded_recovery_states: Freeze disposition, read-only pivot, or blocked guardrail posture while preserving scope and review anchor.

personas.identity_posture: Bounded adjunct posture attached to the owning shell, active review context, and current publication tuple.
personas.visibility_policy_posture: Assistive output inherits the owning shell visibility ceiling and may not widen scope on its own.
primary_jobs_to_be_done: Inspect, accept, reject, or ignore assistive suggestions without displacing primary human-led work.
Use provenance, freshness, and rollout posture to decide whether the assistive output is usable.
allowed_mutations: Visible summary, bounded insert, observe-only review, or feedback capture under the active assistive grant.
continuity_expectations: Remain inside the owning task or ticket shell unless the corpus explicitly calls for standalone evaluation or replay tooling.
degraded_recovery_states: Shadow-only, quarantined, regenerate-required, or blocked posture when rollout, runtime, or trust drifts.
personas.notes: The corpus allows a standalone assistive shell only for evaluation, replay, monitoring, or release-control work; live care and support use stays bounded to the owning shell.
